assertions.expectation: Categorizes notifications by type: auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life/deprecation, security patch
assertions.reason: Response clearly categorizes all four types: auto-upgrade scheduled, new version available, end-of-life/deprecation warnings, and security patches with detailed tables and examples

assertions.expectation: Assigns urgency levels to each category (security patches = highest, new version available = lowest)
assertions.reason: Response uses clear priority levels: IMMEDIATE ACTION for security patches and EoS, PLAN & SCHEDULE for scheduled upgrades, and INFORMATIONAL ONLY for new version available notifications

assertions.expectation: End-of-life warnings require action: plan upgrade before the support window closes
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states EoS enforcement requires immediate action within 0-7 days and provides specific commands for applying maintenance exclusions or upgrading before EoS date

assertions.expectation: Auto-upgrade scheduled notifications should trigger pre-upgrade checklist execution
assertions.reason: Response includes auto-upgrade scheduled in PLAN & SCHEDULE category with specific actions like 'Validate maintenance window timing' and provides playbook with commands to check upgrade info and apply exclusions if needed

assertions.expectation: Security patch notifications may require expedited upgrades outside normal maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response categorizes security patch auto-upgrades as IMMEDIATE ACTION requiring validation of workload compatibility, noting that no action blocks the auto-upgrade from proceeding expeditiously

assertions.expectation: New version available notifications are informational — use for planning, not immediate action
assertions.reason: Response correctly categorizes 'New minor version available' under PLAN & SCHEDULE for planning purposes and 'Patch version available' under INFORMATIONAL ONLY with 'None - auto-upgrades handle this'

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting up Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events
assertions.reason: Response mentions Cloud Logging filters and email alert rules but does not specifically recommend Pub/Sub or Cloud Monitoring alerts for GKE upgrade events

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE release notes as the source for understanding what changed in each version
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE release notes as a source for understanding version changes, focusing instead on triage procedures and command examples
